diagrams.title: RelativeLayout Positioning Model
diagrams.description: A visual representation showing how child views are positioned relative to each other and the parent container in RelativeLayout
elements: Outer rectangle representing parent RelativeLayout container
Inner rectangle A positioned at top-left with android:layout_alignParentTop=true and android:layout_alignParentStart=true
Inner rectangle B positioned below A using android:layout_below=A
Inner rectangle C positioned to the right of B using android:layout_toRightOf=B
Inner rectangle D centered in parent using android:layout_centerInParent=true
Dotted arrows showing relationships: B below A, C to right of B, D centered in parent
Label showing android:layout_width=match_parent and android:layout_height=match_parent on parent
diagrams.purpose: Students learn how to position views relative to each other using attributes like layout_below, layout_toRightOf, layout_alignParentTop, and layout_centerInParent

diagrams.title: TableLayout Structure
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the hierarchical structure of TableLayout with TableRows representing table rows
elements: Large outer rectangle labeled TableLayout
Three nested horizontal rectangles inside labeled TableRow (Row 1), TableRow (Row 2), TableRow (Row 3)
Inside Row 1: Two smaller rectangles labeled TextView (Column 1) and EditText (Column 2)
Inside Row 2: Two smaller rectangles labeled TextView and Button
Inside Row 3: Three smaller rectangles labeled TextView, EditText, Button
Vertical dotted lines showing column alignment across rows
Labels showing android:stretchColumns and android:shrinkColumns attributes
diagrams.purpose: Students understand that TableLayout arranges children in rows and columns, where each TableRow represents a row and children become cells. Also shows how columns align automatically.

diagrams.title: RelativeLayout XML Hierarchy and Attribute Flow
diagrams.description: A flowchart-style diagram showing the XML structure and key positioning attributes in RelativeLayout
elements: Box 1: <RelativeLayout> root element with match_parent dimensions
Arrow to Box 2: Child View 1 with android:id=@+id/view1 and android:layout_alignParentTop=true
Arrow to Box 3: Child View 2 with android:layout_below=@id/view1 and android:layout_centerHorizontal=true
Arrow to Box 4: Child View 3 with android:layout_toRightOf=@id/view2 and android:layout_alignTop=@id/view2
Arrow to Box 5: Child View 4 with android:layout_centerInParent=true and android:layout_margin=16dp
Side panel listing all relative positioning attributes: layout_above, layout_below, layout_toLeftOf, layout_toRightOf, layout_alignStart, layout_alignEnd, layout_alignParentTop/Bottom/Start/End
diagrams.purpose: Students visualize how RelativeLayout XML attributes create positioning relationships between views, reinforcing the parent-child and sibling-sibling positioning rules

diagrams.title: TableLayout Column Stretching and Shrinking
diagrams.description: Visual demonstration of how android:stretchColumns and android:shrinkColumns attributes affect table column display
elements: Left side: Table with stretchColumns=1 showing Column 2 expanded to fill extra space
Right side: Table with shrinkColumns=0,1 showing columns compressed to fit content
Middle section: Table with columnCollapse=true showing columns collapsed initially
Visual indicators showing column widths as bars with arrows
Color coding: Green for stretched columns, Red for shrunk columns
Example with android:layout_span=2 showing column spanning
diagrams.purpose: Students learn to control column behavior using stretchColumns, shrinkColumns, and layout_span attributes to create responsive table layouts

diagrams.title: RelativeLayout and TableLayout Comparison Side-by-Side
diagrams.description: A split diagram comparing the same UI (a login form) implemented in both RelativeLayout and TableLayout
elements: Left panel: Login form in RelativeLayout showing nested linear structures
Right panel: Login form in TableLayout showing clean row-column alignment
Labels on each showing number of nested views
Arrows pointing to key differences: TableLayout provides automatic alignment, RelativeLayout provides more flexible positioning
Bottom section showing XML code snippets for each approach
diagrams.purpose: Students understand when to use each layout type by seeing the same UI implemented both ways, learning that TableLayout excels for tabular data while RelativeLayout offers more positioning freedom

charts.title: RelativeLayout vs TableLayout Comparison
charts.content: Comparison chart with columns: Feature | RelativeLayout | TableLayout | Use Case. Rows: Positioning | Based on relative relationships | Based on row/column grid | RelativeLayout for flexible positioning, TableLayout for structured data. Alignment | Manual via attributes | Automatic column alignment | TableLayout for forms and tables. Nesting | Can nest any layout | Can nest any layout including RelativeLayout | Both support nesting. Performance | Slightly faster | More complex measurement | RelativeLayout slightly better for simple cases. Flexibility | High (any position) | Limited to table structure | RelativeLayout for complex UIs, TableLayout for uniform grids.

charts.title: RelativeLayout Key Attributes Reference
charts.content: Three-column table: Category | Attribute | Description. Parent Relative Positioning: layout_alignParentTop (Aligns top edge with parent top), layout_alignParentBottom (Aligns bottom edge with parent bottom), layout_alignParentStart (Aligns start edge with parent start), layout_alignParentEnd (Aligns end edge with parent end), layout_centerInParent (Centers both horizontally and vertically), layout_centerHorizontal (Centers horizontally), layout_centerVertical (Centers vertically). Sibling Relative Positioning: layout_above (Positions bottom edge above sibling), layout_below (Positions top edge below sibling), layout_toLeftOf (Positions right edge left of sibling), layout_toRightOf (Positions left edge right of sibling), layout_alignTop (Aligns top edge with sibling top), layout_alignBottom (Aligns bottom edge with sibling bottom). Alignment: android:layout_gravity (Gravity within parent), android:layout_margin (Outer spacing), android:layout_padding (Inner spacing)

charts.title: Choosing Between Layouts Flowchart
charts.content: Decision flowchart: Start -> Do you need tabular/grid structure? (Yes -> Use TableLayout, No -> Continue) -> Do you need views positioned relative to each other? (Yes -> Use RelativeLayout, No -> Continue) -> Do you need vertical/horizontal sequential layout? (Yes -> Use LinearLayout, No -> Use FrameLayout or ConstraintLayout). Additional branch: For forms with labels and inputs -> TableLayout recommended. For complex positioning (below, above, left, right of other views) -> RelativeLayout recommended. For equal spacing and sequential arrangement -> LinearLayout recommended.

charts.title: TableLayout Attributes Reference
charts.content: Two-column table with key attributes: android:stretchColumns (Specifies which columns to stretch, accepts column index or comma-separated indices, use * for all columns), android:shrinkColumns (Specifies which columns can shrink, useful for long content), android:collapseColumns (Hides specified columns at runtime), android:layout_span (Applied to child views to span multiple columns), android:layout_column (Specifies which column a view should appear in). Special notes: TableRow children do not have layout_width (defaults to WRAP_CONTENT), TableRow is horizontal LinearLayout by default, Columns are auto-assigned based on child order, Use android:gravity on TableRow to align cells horizontally.

branches: TableLayout - Row and column grid structure
FrameLayout - Stacking views on top of each other
mindMaps.purpose: Provides overview of Android layout managers, positioning RelativeLayout and TableLayout within the broader layout system, helping students understand where these layouts fit among other available options in Android development

branches: RelativeLayout: Parent Positioning (alignParentTop, alignParentBottom, centerInParent), Sibling Positioning (below, above, toLeftOf, toRightOf), Alignment (alignTop, alignBottom, alignStart, alignEnd), Margins and Padding

TableLayout: TableRow (represents each row), Columns (auto-created from children), StretchColumns (expand to fill space), ShrinkColumns (compress to fit), CollapseColumns (hide columns), Column Span (layout_span attribute)

Best Practices: Use TableLayout for forms and data tables, Use RelativeLayout for complex positioned UIs, Avoid deep nesting, Consider ConstraintLayout for new projects, Use match_parent appropriately
mindMaps.purpose: Comprehensive mind map consolidating all key concepts, attributes, and best practices for RelativeLayout and TableLayout, serving as a quick revision aid for exams
